prose:
Frontier-orbital gap of pyridine — DFT, tier `standard` (B3LYP/def2-TZVP, density-fitted) in PySCF 2.x, gas phase, charge 0, multiplicity 1. Geometry: user-supplied Cartesian coordinates from /home/erobles/chem-skills/benchmarks/fidelity/frontier-orbitals-validation/pyridine/pyridine.xyz; no further relaxation (single-point DFT on that geometry, i.e. DFT//input-xyz). SCF converged in 10 cycles. No engine warnings were reported.

Results (orbital energies in eV):
• HOMO = −7.2525992 eV
• LUMO = −1.112786 eV
→ HOMO–LUMO gap = 6.1398131 eV (Koopmans-type orbital gap, not an excitation energy).

Live run log: the calculation streamed to a file of the form `frontier_YYYYMMDD-HHMMSS.out` in the working directory, available for inspection with `tail -f`.

Method provenance block
Property: HOMO–LUMO gap (Koopmans orbital energy difference), eV
Level of theory: B3LYP/def2-TZVP (density-fitted DFT)
Geometry: fixed experimental/benchmark geometry from provided pyridine.xyz; no optimization (single-point)
Environment: gas phase (no implicit solvation)
Charge / multiplicity: 0 / 1 (closed-shell singlet)
Software: PySCF 2.x via chemkit ‘frontier-orbitals’ skill
Convergence: SCF converged (10 cycles, `scf_converged: true`); no warnings reported

All quantities are reported without rounding beyond the precision returned by chemkit.
